channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: On-Device Function Calling in Google AI Edge Gallery
item.description: Google has launched FunctionGemma, a 270M parameter model for mobile devices that enables offline function calling for tasks like calendar management and device control. This innovation enhances user experience by providing fast, efficient AI capabilities directly on devices.

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now features integrations with third-party tools like GitHub and Notion, allowing developers to create more capable AI agents. This ecosystem enhances the functionality and versatility of AI applications.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 is set for May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases AI's potential. This tradition aims to engage participants and highlight the role of AI in enhancing event experiences.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on problem solving, featuring keynotes and specialized tracks on AI advancements. The event emphasizes the importance of in-person collaboration for developers.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, offering improved GPU acceleration and support for PyTorch and JAX. This update aims to enhance the efficiency of deploying generative AI and on-device inference.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Wednesday Build Hour is a new weekly session by Google Cloud experts designed to help developers improve their cloud skills through hands-on activities. This initiative aims to provide practical knowledge and immediate application in workflows.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has released updates to enhance coding workflows, introducing features like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These tools aim to make AI a more tailored and effective collaborator for developers.

item.title: Copilot is ‘for entertainment purposes only,’ according to Microsoft’s terms of use
item.description: Microsoft's terms of use state that its Copilot AI is 'for entertainment purposes only,' cautioning users against blindly trusting AI outputs. This reflects growing concerns about AI reliability and user awareness.

item.title: Can orbital data centers help justify a massive valuation for SpaceX?
item.description: The TechCrunch Equity podcast discusses Elon Musk's vision for orbital data centers and their potential to justify SpaceX's valuation. The conversation explores the implications of this ambitious project for the tech industry.

item.title: In Japan, the robot isn’t coming for your job; it’s filling the one nobody wants
item.description: In Japan, AI is being deployed to address labor shortages by taking on jobs that are less desirable. This shift reflects a growing trend of using technology to fill gaps in the workforce.

item.title: Suno is a music copyright nightmare
item.description: Suno, an AI music platform, claims to prevent the use of copyrighted material but is struggling to enforce this policy effectively. This raises concerns about copyright infringement in AI-generated music.

item.title: gsd-lean 0.14.1
item.description: The gsd-lean 0.14.1 plugin enhances Claude Code with lightweight meta-prompting and specification-driven development features. This update aims to streamline AI programming tasks.

item.title: tensorbored-nightly 2.21.0a20260405
item.description: TensorBored version 2.21.0a20260405 is a PyTorch-first fork of TensorBoard, aimed at enhancing the visualization of tensor data. This update focuses on improving user experience for PyTorch developers.

item.title: forkledger added to PyPI
item.description: Forkledger has been added to PyPI, providing a branch-based decision memory system for AI agents. It allows these agents to store choices and alternatives, enhancing their decision-making capabilities.

item.title: Can Prospects for Nuclear War Get Any Worse? Sure, We Can Put AI in Charge
item.description: The article discusses the increasing concerns about AI's pervasive influence in society, particularly regarding its potential role in nuclear war decision-making. It emphasizes the need for critical discussions about AI's impact.

item.title: ctxl-cli added to PyPI
item.description: The ctxl-cli tool has been added to PyPI, focusing on context engineering to minimize AI agent token waste. It offers a streamlined approach for task-focused instructions and session management.

item.title: tensor-network-visualization 1.5.2
item.description: The tensor-network-visualization 1.5.2 update provides minimal Matplotlib visualizations for various tensor network libraries. This aims to simplify the visualization process for developers working with tensor networks.

item.title: A Resume and Portfolio Presentation Layout for Adobe InDesign That Commands Attention
item.description: A new Adobe InDesign template for resumes and portfolios has been released, featuring a modern design with 12 editable slides. This aims to help users create visually appealing presentations.

item.title: ccr-memory 0.2.4
item.description: The ccr-memory 0.2.4 update introduces persistent memory and self-evolving playbooks for Claude Code, enhancing its capabilities for AI development and experimentation.

item.title: My favorite alternative to Office 365 isn’t LibreOffice – it’s something way cooler
item.description: The article promotes a free alternative to Office 365, suggesting it outperforms LibreOffice and offers better functionality. This challenges the dominance of paid office software solutions.

item.title: goliath-utils-docs 0.1.4
item.description: The goliath-utils 0.1.4 update exposes API documentation for the Goliath-utils library to Claude Code, enhancing developer access to its features.

item.title: vocal-sdk 0.3.8
item.description: The vocal-sdk 0.3.8 is a Python SDK that auto-generates an OpenAI-compatible client for the Vocal API, streamlining integration for developers.

item.title: vocal-ai 0.3.8
item.description: Vocal-ai 0.3.8 offers a self-hosted solution for managing voice models, compatible with OpenAI, catering to developers seeking customizable speech AI.

item.title: Top 10 Best Universities to Study AI in USA 2026 Led by CMU and MIT With Strong Research and Industry Ties
item.description: The demand for AI education in the U.S. is surging, with Carnegie Mellon University and MIT leading the way in 2026 due to their strong research and industry ties.

item.title: Microsoft calls Copilot ‘entertainment only’ while charging $30 a month for it
item.description: Microsoft's Copilot, despite being marketed as an essential AI tool, is labeled as 'entertainment only' in its Terms of Use, raising concerns about its true utility.

item.title: Arynoxtech-AGI added to PyPI
item.description: Arynoxtech-AGI has been added to PyPI, boasting multi-domain capabilities across various industries and claiming superiority over LangChain with its native AGI features.

item.title: Arynoxtech-AGI 2.0.0
item.description: The release of Arynoxtech-AGI 2.0.0 continues to emphasize its multi-domain adaptability and enhanced features, positioning it as a strong competitor in the AGI space.

item.title: For crypto miners turned AI stars, the real test is about to come
item.description: Crypto miners are pivoting to capitalize on AI opportunities, showcasing a shift in focus from traditional mining to AI-driven ventures amid the tech boom.

item.title: Doctor explains when you should worry about fevers, coughs and injuries in children
item.description: Dr. Nighat Arif provides guidance on when parents should be concerned about common health issues in children, emphasizing awareness and timely medical help.

item.title: torchax 0.0.12.dev20260405
item.description: Torchax 0.0.12.dev20260405 is a new library designed to facilitate the integration of Jax and PyTorch, enhancing flexibility for developers working with these frameworks.

item.title: I let Gemini in Google Maps plan my day and it went surprisingly well
item.description: Google's Gemini feature in Maps has been positively received, helping users plan their day effectively. This integration showcases the potential of AI in enhancing user experience in navigation.

item.title: aaws added to PyPI
item.description: A new tool called aaws has been added to PyPI, allowing users to input natural language and receive AWS commands in response. This innovation simplifies cloud management for developers.

item.title: Elon Musk Endorses Bukele on Victims Rights Over Killers in Viral X Post as El Salvador Crime Plummets
item.description: Elon Musk publicly supported El Salvador's President Bukele in a viral post, emphasizing victims' rights over those of criminals as crime rates drop in the country. This endorsement highlights Musk's influence in political discussions.

item.title: ml-networks 0.2.2
item.description: The ml-networks library version 0.2.2 has been released, offering a unified framework for building deep learning models using PyTorch and JAX. This update enhances the development of AI models.

item.title: ml-networks added to PyPI
item.description: ml-networks has been added to PyPI, providing a library for deep learning model construction with a shared configuration system. This addition supports developers in creating AI models more efficiently.

item.title: nvidia-nat-langchain 1.6.0a20260405
item.description: The nvidia-nat-langchain 1.6.0a20260405 package has been released, integrating LangChain with the NeMo Agent Toolkit. This integration enhances natural language processing capabilities in AI applications.

item.title: iMideo – Create cinematic videos from text, images, or clips in minutes
item.description: iMideo is a new tool that allows users to create cinematic videos from text, images, or clips in just minutes. This innovation democratizes video production for creators.

item.title: Recap: Europe’s top funding rounds this week (30 March – 5 April)
item.description: A recap of Europe's top funding rounds highlights significant investments, including Mistral's $830 million debt raise. This reflects the growing ambition and diversity of tech funding in Europe.

item.title: langchain-proofslip added to PyPI
item.description: The langchain-proofslip package has been added to PyPI, providing tools for ephemeral receipt-based verification in AI workflows. This enhances the reliability of AI agents.

item.title: Anthropic launches PAC amid tensions with Trump administration over AI policy
item.description: Anthropic has launched a PAC amid rising tensions with the Trump administration over AI policy, indicating a proactive stance in the regulatory landscape. This move reflects the growing importance of AI governance.

item.title: Zuckerberg's Meta reportedly pauses work with Mercor after $10 billion Silicon Valley startup confirms security breach
item.description: Meta has paused its collaboration with Mercor following a significant security breach linked to a supply-chain attack on the LiteLLM tool, affecting many developers. This incident highlights vulnerabilities in widely-used open-source software.

item.title: Week in review: Axios npm supply chain compromise, critical FortiClient EMS bugs exploited
item.description: The week’s review includes discussions on the Axios npm supply chain compromise and the exploitation of critical bugs in FortiClient EMS, emphasizing the ongoing security challenges in the tech landscape. Financial groups are also strategizing against AI identity attacks.

item.title: Grammarly’s sloppelganger saga
item.description: Grammarly's recent challenges with its AI tools, referred to as the 'sloppelganger saga,' illustrate the complexities and public perceptions surrounding AI-driven writing assistance. The newsletter aims to provide deeper insights into these developments.

item.title: ASUS Zenbook 14 FHD+ OLED Touch Screen Laptop - Intel Core Ultra 7 - 16GB RAM - 512GB SSD - Jasper Gray $699 (1 replies)
item.description: This article provides a product listing for the ASUS Zenbook 14, detailing its specifications and features, including an Intel Core Ultra 7 processor and a 14-inch OLED touchscreen.

item.title: rakib added to PyPI
item.description: The article discusses the addition of 'rakib' to PyPI, focusing on data flow security for AI agents, including provenance tracking and policy enforcement. This development aims to enhance security in AI applications.

item.title: Indian film studios are using AI to cut costs, speed production, despite mixed audience reactions - The Economic Times
item.description: Indian film studios are increasingly using AI to reduce production costs and accelerate filmmaking, utilizing AI for dubbing across multiple languages despite mixed audience reactions. This trend signifies a shift in the industry’s approach to technology.

item.title: Unverified: What Practitioners Post About OCR, Agents, and Tables
item.description: Anonymous practitioners share unverified insights on OCR accuracy, agent failures, and table extraction, revealing consistent patterns in their experiences. This highlights the challenges faced in AI implementation.

item.title: Introducing the Agent Governance Toolkit: Open-source runtime security for AI agents | Microsoft Open Source Blog
item.description: Microsoft has introduced the Agent Governance Toolkit, designed to enhance policy, identity, and reliability for autonomous AI agents, aiming to improve security and governance in AI applications.

item.title: Britain woos Anthropic expansion after US defence clash, FT says
item.description: Following a clash with the US government over national security concerns, Britain is courting Anthropic for expansion, as the company has been blacklisted for refusing military use of its AI chatbot, Claude.

item.title: Samsung, Mistral AI discuss cooperation in AI memory sector
item.description: Samsung Electronics and Mistral AI are exploring potential cooperation in the AI memory sector, following a meeting between their executives. This collaboration could enhance advancements in semiconductor technology.
